waypoints.narration: Schaeffer invented musique concrète in Paris, proving that recorded sounds themselves could be musical material.
Stockhausen pushed electronic composition into uncharted territory at the Cologne studio, inspiring generations.
Derbyshire realized the iconic Doctor Who theme at the BBC Radiophonic Workshop, pioneering electronic sound design.
Carlos brought the Moog synthesizer to mainstream ears with Switched-On Bach, bridging classical and electronic.
As Kraftwerk, Hütter and Schneider created the blueprint for electronic pop, techno, and synth music.
Eno coined 'ambient music' and revolutionized studio production, shaping artists from Bowie to U2.
Atkins fused Kraftwerk's electronic precision with Detroit's soul to create techno — a genre that conquered the world.
